ideas: Continuously refine AI agents and workflows based on health reports, production telemetry, and user feedback.
Keep homepage and key landing pages transparently advertising the autonomous improvement stack so visitors understand that Zion is self-optimizing.
Regularly add new autonomous-operations ideas (new agents, workflows, and reporting views) into this backlog so the system never runs out of evolution paths.
quickWins: Add schema.org FAQ markup to key pages
newIdeas: Expose a public-facing overview of Zion's autonomous AI improvement agents, including continuous app evolution, site health monitoring, and CI/CD recovery flows
Instrument homepage CTAs with richer telemetry so autonomous agents can prioritize improvements based on real engagement
Add a self-description panel where Zion's AI agents explain, in plain language, what they improved in the last week on the site
Introduce a lightweight in-app feedback hook that routes user feedback into the evolution backlog for agents to process
Add testimonials section with social proof
Implement dark mode toggle for accessibility
Add newsletter signup with value proposition

[Layout/Design] Consider grouping or summarizing lower-page sections: The homepage contains a large number of sections and headings. Consider turning some repeated patterns into carousels, tabs, or collapsible sections so decision-makers can scan the page more comfortably.

evolutionRoadmap: Deepen homepage launch roadmap content to better explain 2–4 week pilot and production hardening phases
Expand AI Services overview page to mirror Advanced AI, Core Services, and Industry Solutions pillars from the live site
Create a dedicated Automation & AI Agents page describing the continuous-improvement stack (development agents, site improvement agent, ACIA, and orchestrators) with links from the homepage
Gradually expand autonomous agents' scope to include more nuanced UX experiments (copy variants, layout adjustments) gated by health scores and CI checks
Teach the AI Continuous Improvement Agent to read its own reports and automatically tighten or relax scopes (PRIORITY_MODE, IMPROVEMENT_SCOPE) based on recent health scores
Create an autonomous documentation updater that summarizes major agent-driven changes and surfaces them to the new Automation & AI Agents page
